query:
Sec review of the Issue 444 fix.  your thoughts? F-1 (low, non-blocking): heal_pass_batch_size missing range enforcement in validate()

Location: crates/unimatrix-server/src/infra/config.rs

The field comment documents "Valid range: [1, 1000]" but InferenceConfig::validate() does not enforce it. Every other bounded field in this struct has a corresponding check (e.g., max_graph_inference_per_tick [1, 1000], graph_inference_k [1, 100], query_log_lookback_days [1, 3650]). A user who sets heal_pass_batch_size = 0 will bind LIMIT 0 to the heal-pass query, silently disabling it — contrary to documented intent and without any diagnostic signal at startup.

The failure mode is benign (heal pass skips silently rather than crashing), so this is non-blocking. Recommend adding a >= 1 (and optionally <= 1000) check inside validate() in a follow-up, consistent with the procedure documented in entry #3759.

F-2 (low, non-blocking): New SQL queries use bare integer literals for status discriminants

Location: crates/unimatrix-server/src/services/status.rs lines 699, 937, 986

New queries use status = 0 and status = 3 as bare literals. The Status enum carries #[repr(u8)] with stable discriminants, so there is no correctness risk today. The established codebase pattern binds via .bind(Status::Active as u8 as i64) (e.g., read.rs:1168) to make the coupling explicit and catch future enum changes at compile time. No security implication; code hygiene observation only.

entries.title: Security reviewer rated InferenceConfig validate() gap and bare SQL status literals as non-blocking; human upgraded both to blocking — rework required
Pool selection for read-only context_status aggregates: always check compute_status_aggregates as direct precedent
run_maintenance() tick order: prune → heal → compact (never reverse)
co_access promotion SELECT missing quarantine-status filter causes compaction/promotion oscillation
ADR-002 correction: prune pass and heal pass are also unconditional since bugfix-444 (supersedes #3559, #178)
